meta.note: Nicaragua + Guatemala + Dominican Republic sweep, published books. New country row added for the Dominican Republic. Nicaragua: Ortega-era censorship (Belli) + Somoza-era Sandino-book prohibitions (from Sergio Ramírez's 'Libros prohibidos' essay). Guatemala: 1954 anti-communist confiscation lists (El Faro) + Asturias's Week-end en Guatemala. Dominican Republic: Trujillo-era suppressions (de facto, no per-title decree — confidence set accordingly). Some Sandino-book ban years are pre-1979 proxies (1960), noted.

entries.title: Un silencio lleno de murmullos
entries.description_ban: Belli's novel was detained at Managua customs in April 2026 and barred from entry; the author, stripped of Nicaraguan citizenship in 2023, called it state censorship.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific customs ban; reported by Infobae and La Prensa.

entries.title: Sandino
entries.description_ban: A shipment of 5,000 copies of Macaulay's biography of Sandino was detained at Managua customs under the Somoza regime, as recounted in Sergio Ramírez's essay 'Libros prohibidos'.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific customs seizure; documented by Listín Diario (Sergio Ramírez essay).

entries.title: Sandino, general de hombres libres
entries.description_ban: Selser's classic Sandino biography was prohibited from entry and circulation under Somoza and circulated clandestinely in mimeographed copies; one distributor was jailed six months. Ban year is a Somoza-era proxy.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific prohibition; documented by Listín Diario (Ramírez) and Nicaraguan press.

entries.title: Con Sandino en Nicaragua
entries.description_ban: Part of the blanket Somoza-era prohibition on Sandino books; entry and circulation were barred until the 1979 Sandinista triumph. Ban year is a Somoza-era proxy.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific prohibition within the Somoza-era Sandino-book ban (medium confidence).

entries.title: Efemérides de los hechos notables acaecidos en la República de Centro-América
entries.description_ban: A 19th-century Central American chronicle that appeared on the confiscation lists of the anti-communist commission after the 1954 coup — an example of the purge's indiscriminate 'communist' labelling.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific 1954 confiscation; documented by El Faro.

entries.title: Guía ejemplificada para medir el rendimiento escolar
entries.description_ban: An educational measurement manual that nonetheless appeared on the 1954 anti-communist commission's confiscation lists — cited as evidence of the purge's indiscriminate criteria.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific 1954 confiscation; documented by El Faro.

entries.title: Week-end en Guatemala
entries.description_ban: Asturias's story collection condemning the 1954 CIA-backed coup, written in exile after Castillo Armas stripped his citizenship; barred from circulation under the regime's prohibition of works deemed communist.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban within the post-1954 censorship of Asturias's work (medium confidence).

entries.title: La era de Trujillo
entries.description_ban: Galíndez's doctoral study of the Trujillo regime; Trujillo had advance knowledge of it and had Galíndez kidnapped from New York and killed in 1956. The book could not circulate inside the Dominican Republic.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Named among 'the prohibited books of the Trujillo dictatorship'; documented by El Día (de facto ban, medium confidence).

entries.title: Cementerio sin cruces
entries.description_ban: Anti-Trujillo novel published in Mexico; not distributed on the island, and its author was assassinated by a Trujillo agent in New York in 1952. Listed among the dictatorship's prohibited books.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Named among the Trujillo-era prohibited books; documented by El Día and Acento (de facto ban, medium confidence).

entries.title: Una Gestapo en América
entries.description_ban: Exile-published account of torture at the Nigua prison under Trujillo; named in El Día's roster of books prohibited under the dictatorship and barred from circulation inside the country.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Named among the Trujillo-era prohibited books; documented by El Día (de facto ban, low confidence).
